items.why_it_matters: A user who names the actual appliance direction is much easier to route into a useful next step than someone who only says they want appliances.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the project direction and keep gathering timing plus first-visit facts.

items.why_it_matters: Timing is one of the clearest signs that a visit inquiry is moving toward action.
items.echoer_action: Keep the reply warm and direct the next step toward visit facts.

items.why_it_matters: First-time caution means the reply should optimize for reassurance and trust, not for closing speed.
items.echoer_action: Use calm language and guide toward a visit-style next step.

items.why_it_matters: Deadline-driven visit requests often convert quickly if slot and service fit are handled well.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge urgency and ask for deadline plus available slot.

items.why_it_matters: Price-only questions are not low intent by default, but they need scope before the next step becomes useful.
items.echoer_action: Answer lightly and recover one scope detail.

items.why_it_matters: When the concern is personal and exposed in public, the response must protect dignity while staying useful.
items.echoer_action: Reply gently, avoid echoing the shame language, and guide toward a safer next step.
